citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
One female mini poodle (microchip #94169, Tag 13) was identified by the inspector as being severely underweight. The
dog born on 7/19/2025 had been placed into a heated indoor housing area after it was found to be unable to re-enter the
heated portion of the run earlier this morning at approximately 7:30 AM. The flap door to the entrance of the heated area
was not obstructed. The facility representative had not identified the animal as being underweight or requiring veterinary
care. At the time of the inspection the vet had not been contacted. The dogs' neck is extremely thin, the spinal vertebrae
are clearly observed, and the pelvic bones are protruding. When the inspector physically examined the dog her spine,
hips, and ribs were easily felt under the haircoat.
Failure to adequately observe animals daily to identify veterinary care conditions or communicating veterinary issues to a
veterinarian can lead to worsening of the condition, delay in treatments, or death. Correct by ensuring that the animals
and conditions listed above are reported to the attending veterinarian so that the animal can be properly examined,
diagnosed, and treated. Additionally, ensure that daily observation of animals is adequate to identify those signs of
abnormal health and behavior and that there is direct and frequent communication of those observations to the attending
veterinarian. Correct by 5:00 PM on 08-April-2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
One female mini poodle (microchip #94169, Tag 13) was identified by the inspector as being severely underweight. The 
dog born on 7/19/2025 had been placed into a heated indoor housing area after it was found to be unable to re-enter the 
heated portion of the run earlier this morning at approximately 7:30 AM. The flap door to the entrance of the heated area 
was not obstructed. The facility representative had not identified the animal as being underweight or requiring veterinary 
care. At the time of the inspection the vet had not been contacted. The dogs' neck is extremely thin, the spinal vertebrae 
are clearly observed, and the pelvic bones are protruding. When the inspector physically examined the dog her spine, 
hips, and ribs were easily felt under the haircoat. 
 
Failure to adequately observe animals daily to identify veterinary care conditions or communicating veterinary issues to a 
veterinarian can lead to worsening of the condition, delay in treatments, or death. Correct by ensuring that the animals 
and conditions listed above are reported to the attending veterinarian so that the animal can be properly examined, 
diagnosed, and treated. Additionally, ensure that daily observation of animals is adequate to identify those signs of 
abnormal health and behavior and that there is direct and frequent communication of those observations to the attending 
veterinarian. Correct by 5:00 PM on 08-April-2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
